findings.summary: current_sense detector produces 68 false positives in glasgow.kicad_sch by pairing R49 (0.15 ohm shunt) with every IC sharing GND net

correct.description: R49 (0R15) + INA233 current sense IC is correctly detected

incorrect.description: 67 of 68 current_sense detections are false positives — R49 paired with unrelated ICs (SN74LVC1T45, CAT24M01X, etc.) that merely share GND net

suggestions: Detector should require shunt resistor to be in series path (not shunt to GND) or check sense IC has current sense pins (INP/INM)

findings.summary: Voltage divider false positives: R30/R48 and R56/R48 on ISNS_H net are current sense network components, not dividers. R5/R4 on ~{CY_RESET} is a pull-up/pull-down pair, not a sensing divider.

correct.description: R33/R28 (49k9/59k) feedback divider for VDAC→VFB correctly detected with is_feedback=True
R34/R35 on VIO→VFB also appears to be a legitimate feedback divider

incorrect.description: R30 (4R7) / R48 (0R15) on ISNS_H → GND detected as divider but these are current sense shunt resistors
R56 (0R33) / R48 (0R15) on ISNS_H → GND same issue — current sense, not divider
R5/R4 (100k/100k) on ~{CY_RESET} → GND is a pull-up/pull-down pair on a digital reset line, not a voltage sensing divider

suggestions: Filter out sub-1-ohm resistor pairs (these are current sense). Filter out equal-value resistor pairs on digital signal names. Require mid-point connection to an analog input or feedback pin.

findings.summary: CRITICAL: +3V3 power rail completely missing from nets dict despite 52 power symbols in main sheet + 33 in io_banks (85 total). GND (133 symbols), +5V (10), +1V2 (5) all work correctly. This means ALL +3V3 connections are invisible — signal analysis for anything on the 3.3V rail will fail.

correct.description: GND, +5V, +1V2, VCCPLL0, VCCPLL1, GNDPLL0, GNDPLL1 all correctly appear in nets

suggestions: Debug build_net_map() for +3V3 specifically. May be a naming collision, coordinate issue, or power symbol lib_id resolution problem. Check if +3V3 power symbols have correct pin positions.
related_issues: NEW - not yet in issue tracker

findings.summary: 73 capacitors with value "u1" (European notation for 0.1uF) have no parsed_value. Parser handles "4u7" but fails when unit prefix precedes digit.

missed.description: "u1" = 0.1uF not parsed. Pattern: unit prefix before digit (u1, n47, p33). Parser only handles digit-first like "4u7".

suggestions: Add parsing for prefix-first notation: u1=0.1u, n47=0.47n, p33=0.33p

findings.summary: Gerber review: 6 revisions (revA-C3) of 4-layer FPGA board. Cross-revision progression correctly tracked

correct.description: All 6 revisions correctly 4-layer. Alignment progression accurate: revA-C1 misaligned, revC2-C3 aligned. Via evolution (0.3mm-only to 0.3+0.2mm BGA escape) captured
revC3 correctly uses X2 classification (KiCad 7) while revA-C2 use diameter heuristic (KiCad 5.x). Correctly adapts across versions
Growing file count handled: 11 (revA) to 15 (revC3, adds Fab+Courtyard). Extra layers not false-flagged

incorrect.description: smd_ratio=0.0 for all 6 revisions. Wrong for FPGA board (revC3: 812 front paste flashes)
revC3: 3.5mm mounting holes misclassified as component_holes via X2 ComponentDrill. Same holes correctly classified in revC1/C2 via diameter heuristic
has_pth_drill=false for revA-C2 (5 revisions) despite 189-560 vias. Only revC3 with explicit MixedPlating gets true

suggestions: Override X2 ComponentDrill for NPTH >= 2.5mm as mounting holes
